description: Main accounting definitions and formulas that are not part of the units, physics, math or chemistry base data.

words.name: accounting
words.description: measurement, processing and communication of financial information about economic entities

words.name: auditing
words.description: systematic and independent examination of books, accounts, documents and vouchers of an organization

words.name: bookkeeping
words.description: recording and picking up business transactions

words.name: accrual
words.description: in finance, adding together of interest or different investments over a period of time

words.name: dividend
words.description: payment made by a corporation to its shareholders to distribute profits

words.name: inventory
words.description: goods held for resale, or raw materials stocked to produce those goods

words.name: goodwill
words.description: accounting term for the intangible asset recognized when a firm is purchased as a going concern

words.name: expense
words.description: use or service to be provided to achieve a certain benefit

words.name: EBITDA
words.description: accounting measure: net earnings, before interest expenses, taxes, depreciation, and amortization are subtracted

words.name: amortization
words.description: accounting term for the spreading of payments over multiple periods

words.name: write-off
words.description: reduction in recognized value of an entity

words.name: provision
words.description: account which records a present liability of an entity

words.name: account
words.description: central data structure in the practice of accounting

words.name: ledger
words.description: book registering economic transactions

words.name: invoice
words.description: commercial document issued by a seller to a buyer, relating to a sale transaction and indicating the products, quantities, and agreed prices for products or services the seller has provided the buyer

words.name: overhead
words.description: ongoing operating expense which do not directly generate profits, but which is vital to to profit making

words.name: payroll
words.description: list of a company's employees and the amount of money they are to be paid

words.name: solvency
words.description: financial term

words.name: leverage
words.description: the use of borrowed funds rather than fresh equity in the purchase of an asset

words.name: budget
words.description: balance sheet or statement of estimated receipts and expenditures

words.name: creditor
words.description: person or organization that has a right to a benefit

words.name: debtor
words.description: natural or legal person who enters into a debt relationship; entity that owes a debt to another entity

words.name: shareholder
words.description: individual or organization that owns part of a corporation through shares of its stock

triples.name: financial accounting
triples.description: field of accounting concerned with the summary, analysis and reporting of financial transactions related to a business. see Wikipedia: Financial accounting (Q949180)

triples.name: management accounting
triples.description: field of business administration, part of the internal accounting system of a company. see Wikipedia: Management accounting (Q2990807)

triples.name: cost accounting
triples.description: systematic set of procedures for recording and reporting measurements of the cost of manufacturing goods and performing services in the aggregate and in detail. see Wikipedia: Cost accounting (Q1077518)

triples.name: forensic accounting
triples.description: accounting of engagements from disputes or litigation which have or are expected to happen. see Wikipedia: Forensic accounting (Q2663158)

triples.name: balance sheet
triples.description: accounting financial summary. see Wikipedia: Balance sheet (Q46834)

triples.name: income statement
triples.description: financial statement of a company: shows the company's revenues and expenses during a particular period. see Wikipedia: Income statement (Q243460)

triples.name: cash flow statement
triples.description: financial statement that shows how changes in balance sheet accounts and income affect cash and cash equivalents, and breaks the analysis down to operating, investing and financing activities. see Wikipedia: Cash flow statement (Q460184)

triples.name: financial statement
triples.description: formal record of the financial activities and position of a business, person, or other entity. see Wikipedia: Financial statement (Q192907)

triples.name: accounting equation
triples.description: fundamental equation of the double-entry bookkeeping model. see Wikipedia: Accounting equation (Q864368)

triples.name: basis of accounting
triples.description: time when financial transactions are reported. see Wikipedia: Basis of accounting (Q832927)

triples.name: matching principle
triples.description: accounting method. see Wikipedia: Matching principle (Q4388479)

triples.name: revenue recognition
triples.description: accounting method. see Wikipedia: Revenue recognition (Q2146785)

triples.name: going concern
triples.description: business not under threat of substantially curtailing its operation. see Wikipedia: Going concern (Q565043)

triples.name: accounting period
triples.description: accounting method. see Wikipedia: Accounting period (Q1754678)

triples.name: fiscal year
triples.description: one-year term for government and business financial reporting. see Wikipedia: Fiscal year (Q191891)

triples.name: retained earnings
triples.description: accumulated net income (or loss) of the corporation that is retained by the corporation. see Wikipedia: Retained earnings (Q240691)

triples.name: share capital
triples.description: portion of a company's equity that has been obtained by trading stock to a shareholder for cash. see Wikipedia: Share capital (Q330601)

triples.name: common stock
triples.description: form of corporate equity ownership; a type of security. see Wikipedia: Common stock (Q1081659)

triples.name: book value
triples.description: in accounting, the value of an asset according to its balance sheet account balance. see Wikipedia: Book value (Q1761731)

triples.name: accounts payable
triples.description: money owed by a business to its suppliers. see Wikipedia: Accounts payable (Q250058)

triples.name: accounts receivable
triples.description: legally enforceable claim for payment to a business by its customer/ clients for goods supplied and/or services rendered in execution of the customer's order. see Wikipedia: Accounts receivable (Q328554)

triples.name: fixed asset
triples.description: assets and property that cannot easily be converted into cash, like property, plant or equipment. see Wikipedia: Fixed asset (Q955421)

triples.name: current asset
triples.description: an asset which can either be converted to cash or used to pay current liabilities within 12 months. see Wikipedia: Current asset (Q730884)

triples.name: current liability
triples.description: all liabilities of the business that are to be settled in cash within the fiscal year or the operating cycle of a given firm. see Wikipedia: Current liability (Q2353682)

triples.name: intangible asset
triples.description: asset that lacks physical substance and usually is very hard to evaluate. see Wikipedia: Intangible asset (Q427581)

triples.name: net income
triples.description: measure of the profitability of a business venture. see Wikipedia: Net income (Q570938)

triples.name: gross profit
triples.description: sum of all earnings before taxes. see Wikipedia: Gross income (Q3683077)

triples.name: operating expense
triples.description: ongoing cost for running a product, business, or system. see Wikipedia: Operating expense (Q831940)

triples.name: earnings before interest and taxes
triples.description: measure of a firm's profit. see Wikipedia: Earnings before interest and taxes (Q720862)

triples.name: accumulated depreciation
triples.description: allocating the cost of a (tangible) asset over a period of time. see Wikipedia: Depreciation (Q114403)

triples.name: salvage value
triples.description: one of the constituents of a leasing calculus which describes the future value of a good in terms of absolute value. see Wikipedia: Residual value (Q754849)

triples.name: deferred income
triples.description: term in accounting. see Wikipedia: Deferred income (Q885383)

triples.name: bad debt
triples.description: monetary amount owed to a creditor that is unlikely to be paid. see Wikipedia: Bad debt (Q1365583)

triples.name: capital expenditure
triples.description: costs associated with the procurement and maintenance of, and improvements to, the fixed assets of an organisation. see Wikipedia: Capital expenditure (Q302208)

triples.name: earnings per share
triples.description: value of earnings per outstanding share of common stock for a company. see Wikipedia: Earnings per share (Q1134378)

triples.name: accrued liability
triples.description: an expense that has been incurred but not yet paid or recorded in the accounts at the end of a period. see Wikipedia: Accrued liabilities (Q4672887)

triples.name: historical cost
triples.description: original monetary value of an item. see Wikipedia: Historical cost (Q362098)

triples.name: tangible property
triples.description: property which can be touched. see Wikipedia: Tangible property (Q7682959)

triples.name: price-earnings ratio
triples.description: the ratio of a company's share price to the company's earnings per share. see Wikipedia: Price-earnings ratio (Q127473)

triples.name: general ledger
triples.description: central repository for accounting data. see Wikipedia: General ledger (Q1977520)

triples.name: general journal
triples.description: volume used in bookkeeping containing daily records of receipts and expenditures in the order of their occurrence. see Wikipedia: General journal (Q608971)

triples.name: journal entry
triples.description: logging of a transaction into accounting journal items. see Wikipedia: Journal entry (Q3935376)

triples.name: double-entry bookkeeping
triples.description: seamless, chronological and factual ordered recording of all business processes in a company based of documented evidence. see Wikipedia: Double-entry bookkeeping (Q192803)

triples.name: debits and credits
triples.description: amounts added or subtracted to account ledgers. see Wikipedia: Debits and credits (Q1120961)

triples.name: chart of accounts
triples.description: list of financial accounts used in the organization's general ledger. see Wikipedia: Chart of accounts (Q1406649)

triples.name: trial balance
triples.description: list of all business accounts in a ledger. see Wikipedia: Trial balance (Q1192485)

triples.name: financial transaction
triples.description: agreement, or communication, carried out between a buyer and a seller to exchange an asset for payment. see Wikipedia: Financial transaction (Q1166072)

triples.name: petty cash
triples.description: proxy. see Wikipedia: Petty cash (Q3523818)

triples.name: bank reconciliation
triples.description: accounting term. see Wikipedia: Reconciliation (accounting) (Q332836)

triples.name: operating margin
triples.description: relating operating profits to net sales. see Wikipedia: Operating margin (Q722249)

triples.name: working capital
triples.description: financial metric. see Wikipedia: Working capital (Q823288)

triples.name: current ratio
triples.description: liquidity ratio that measures whether or not a firm has enough resources to meet its short-term obligations. see Wikipedia: Current ratio (Q2210989)

triples.name: quick ratio
triples.description: company's liquid assets divided by its current liabilities. see Wikipedia: Quick ratio (Q1310347)

triples.name: debt-to-equity ratio
triples.description: financial ratio indicating the relative proportion of shareholders' equity and debt used to finance a company's assets. see Wikipedia: Debt-to-equity ratio (Q167064)

triples.name: accounting liquidity
triples.description: measure of the ability of a debtor to pay their debts as and when they fall due. see Wikipedia: Accounting liquidity (Q1827667)

triples.name: asset turnover
triples.description: sales generated per dollar of assets. see Wikipedia: Asset turnover (Q1728600)

triples.name: cost center
triples.description: management accounting term. see Wikipedia: Cost centre (business) (Q1785269)

triples.name: internal control
triples.description: process or system used by an organization to manage risk and diminish the occurrence of fraud. see Wikipedia: Internal control (Q1667931)

triples.name: accounting standard
triples.description: the specific accounting discipline followed (IFRS, US GAAP, Chinese AS, France GAAP, Germany GAAP, Russian GAAP, UK GAAP, etc.). see Wikipedia: Accounting standard (Q1779838)

triples.name: International Financial Reporting Standards
triples.description: accounting standard issued by the IFRS Foundation. see Wikipedia: International Financial Reporting Standards (Q176831)

triples.name: financial audit
triples.description: audit conducted to ensure financial statements are in accordance with specified criteria. see Wikipedia: Financial audit (Q740419)

triples.name: FIFO and LIFO accounting
triples.description: method in managing inventory and financial matters. see Wikipedia: FIFO and LIFO accounting (Q1265942)

triples.name: useful life
triples.description: the estimated period over which a fixed asset is expected to be usable by a business

triples.name: number of shares
triples.description: the count of shares of common stock outstanding

triples.name: beginning retained earnings
triples.description: the balance of retained earnings at the start of an accounting period

triples.name: commercial year
triples.description: a year of 360 days (twelve 30-day months) used by convention in interest and accounting calculations

sources.name: by definition
sources.description: a value that is fixed by an unambiguous definition or convention rather than measured
